title: Weak Cryptography & Insecure Randomness
principle: Use current, vetted primitives and let a library pick the details. For integrity use SHA-256 or better, never MD5 or SHA-1. For encryption use an authenticated mode like AES-GCM, never ECB, and never a hardcoded key or IV. For anything security-relevant — tokens, IDs, salts — draw from a cryptographically secure RNG, not the language's default random.

snippets.note: MD5/SHA-1 are broken for security use. random is predictable; secrets (or os.urandom) is the CSPRNG. For passwords use argon2/bcrypt, not a bare hash.
Math.random() is not cryptographic — never use it for tokens, session ids, or password reset codes. Use crypto.randomBytes / randomUUID.
ECB leaks structure; GCM authenticates. SecureRandom, not Random, for keys/tokens. Never hardcode the key or reuse a GCM nonce.

snippets.secure:
sum := sha256.Sum256(data)
b := make([]byte, 32); crypto_rand.Read(b)  // crypto/rand
snippets.note: math/rand is deterministic and seedable — never for secrets. Use crypto/rand. For encryption use cipher.NewGCM, not a raw block mode.

references.title: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
